name: Big Bill Broonzy
description: American blues singer, songwriter and guitarist (1893–1958)
abstract: Big Bill Broonzy (born Lee Conley Bradley; June 26, 1893 or 1903 – August 14, 1958), later known as William Lee Broonzy, was an American blues singer, songwriter, and guitarist. His career began in the 1920s, when he played country music to mostly African-American audiences.

about_quotes.speaker: Ann Rabson
about_quotes.evidence: I never got to see him live. Never. But he was live on the radio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ann Rabson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 21, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Ann Rabson
about_quotes.evidence: I heard Big Bill Broonzy playing live on the radio... And it really changed my life.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ann Rabson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 21, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: Buster had this record date for this guy named Melrose Mellow's, and I didn't realize who the fellow was -- Big Bill -- we made a record called "WPA" ... Big Bill Broonzy. It was all blues dates.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Art Hodes
about_quotes.evidence: I always remember, Big Bill Broonzy and Wild Bill Davison and going out and getting a fifth of booze... And Wild Bill had a drink and then Big Bill grabbed the bottle.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Had Big Bill from Chicago... Big Bill had on some tight shoes were hurtin' his feet. He would stand up on the stage playin' and cryin'.

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...he also wrote that book on Big Bill Broonzy! Do you know who I mean. [INTERVIEWEE] I know who you mean.
